summary: Dan Shapiro recounts a painful workshop injury sustained while building a dining room table. While tightening pipe clamps on a glue-up, a crescent wrench slipped and slammed his fingernail into a neighboring clamp. He had to finish clamping before the glue dried, then visited a doctor who laughed at his question about losing the nail. The post serves as a cautionary tale that hand tools can be just as dangerous as power tools.
key_points: A crescent wrench slip during a table glue-up caused a serious fingernail injury requiring a hand specialist
Time pressure from drying glue forced him to finish the job before seeking medical attention
Hand tools deserve the same respect and caution as power tools
money_quotes: When I asked the doctor if I was going to lose the nail, he laughed at me.
You need to be every bit as careful with hand tools as you do with power tools.
I can't believe I did all this damage to my hand with a crescent wrench!
The glue was drying, so I had to finish tightening the other six clamps before I could haul my butt to the doctor's office.
I spent the whole weekend taking Vicodin and playing Baldur's Gate (fortunately the ring finger isn't used on the xbox controller).
key_insight: Hand tools deserve the same respect as power tools — complacency around seemingly safe equipment can still lead to serious injury.
